04-03.headline: Ken Griffey Jr. makes his major league debut at 19
04-03.story: In his first big-league at-bat, the 19-year-old center fielder doubles off Dave Stewart in Oakland. It is the first hit of a Mariners career that will include 417 home runs, ten Gold Gloves, and the franchise's first Hall of Fame plaque.

04-06.headline: The Mariners play their first game
04-06.story: Baseball returns to Seattle as 57,762 fill the Kingdome for the franchise's first game. Diego Segui throws the first pitch in club history, and the Mariners fall 7-0 to the California Angels — the first entry in a ledger that now spans five decades.

05-02.headline: Mike Cameron hits four home runs in one game
05-02.story: Cameron homers in each of his first four at-bats in Chicago, joining one of baseball's rarest clubs. He nearly makes it five, sending a fifth ball to the warning track before settling for a walk. No Mariner has matched the feat since.

06-02.headline: Randy Johnson throws the first no-hitter in franchise history
06-02.story: Fourteen seasons into their existence, the Mariners finally have a no-hitter. Johnson walks six Tigers and strikes out eight at the Kingdome, holding Detroit hitless in a 2-0 win. It is the first of what will become a Hall of Fame résumé's many milestones.

06-08.headline: Six pitchers combine to no-hit the Dodgers
06-08.story: Kevin Millwood starts it, and five relievers finish it: the first combined no-hitter in Mariners history, 1-0 over Los Angeles at Safeco Field. Six pitchers sharing one line in the record book remains a club original.

07-13.headline: Ichiro signs a five-year, $90 million extension
07-13.story: Three days after winning the All-Star Game MVP with an inside-the-park home run, Ichiro Suzuki commits his prime to Seattle. The extension keeps the franchise cornerstone in a Mariners uniform through 2012 and off that fall's free-agent market.

08-12.headline: Félix Hernández throws a perfect game
08-12.story: Twenty-seven Rays up, twenty-seven down. Félix strikes out twelve and finishes the first perfect game in Mariners history, dropping to his knees as Safeco Field erupts. The final line: 1-0, on a single run the King made stand for nine innings.

08-31.headline: Griffey Sr. and Jr. become the first father-son teammates
08-31.story: For the first time in major league history, a father and son take the field together. Batting back-to-back in the lineup, Ken Griffey Sr. and Ken Griffey Jr. each single in the first inning against Kansas City at the Kingdome.

09-14.headline: The Griffeys hit back-to-back home runs
09-14.story: Two weeks after becoming teammates, Ken Griffey Sr. and Ken Griffey Jr. hit back-to-back home runs off Kirk McCaskill in Anaheim — father first, son right behind him. It has never happened before or since.

10-01.headline: Ichiro breaks the single-season hits record
10-01.story: Ichiro Suzuki singles off Ryan Drese of Texas for hit number 258, passing George Sisler's mark that had stood since 1920. The Safeco Field crowd calls him out of the dugout twice; Sisler's family, in the stands, stands with them.

10-08.headline: Edgar hits The Double
10-08.story: Bottom of the 11th, Game 5 of the ALDS, season on the line. Edgar Martínez lines a double down the left-field line, Joey Cora scores, and Ken Griffey Jr. comes all the way around from first to beat the Yankees 6-5. The play that saved baseball in Seattle needs only two words.

09-30.headline: Cal Raleigh ends the drought
09-30.story: Pinch-hitting in the bottom of the ninth of a scoreless game, Raleigh homers off Oakland's Domingo Acevedo to clinch the Mariners' first playoff berth since 2001. Twenty-one years of waiting end on one swing.
